content: Phase III trial design uses rigorous randomized controlled methodology with adequate statistical power (typically 90% power, alpha 0.05). Superiority trials test whether the investigational drug is statistically better than placebo or active comparator on primary endpoint(s). Non-inferiority trials establish that the new drug is not unacceptably worse (within a pre-specified margin) than standard treatment, often used when placebo is unethical. Designs include parallel-group, factorial, or crossover. Endpoints must be clinically meaningful and validated. Intention-to-treat analysis is primary. Interim analyses with data monitoring committees ensure patient safety and allow early stopping for overwhelming efficacy or futility.
